implementation_now_policy: No finding may be implemented in this contour.
canon_arbitration: If visual fidelity conflicts with command truth, command truth wins.
If UI ambition conflicts with current COMMAND_API, current COMMAND_API wins.
If a finding requires implementation, it is recorded as a future contour item.
If a finding touches runtime behavior, it is out of scope for this contour.

findings.title: Overview action ledger creates 1600x1000 viewport density pressure.

evidence.observed: At 1600x1000 the own-UI screenshot shows the action panel consuming vertical space and the lower overview content partially below the visible window. Source-design comparison was reviewed outside repo, but this finding is supported by the own-UI screenshot and current layout refs only.

canon_basis: MASTER_PLAN.md:192 requires truthful UI visibility of mode, endpoint, health, and pool state.
WORKFLOW_OS_V1_2.md:452 through WORKFLOW_OS_V1_2.md:462 block design polish here; this contour may only record the gap.

findings.title: Overview header mixes fixture controls with operator actions and diverges structurally from the source shell.

evidence.observed: The current prototype header includes source/fixture selectors and several direct action buttons. This is useful for verification, but it is not structurally aligned with the cleaner source design header.

canon_basis: MASTER_PLAN.md:175 says the GUI value is policy layer, truthful runtime status, staged scaling, and operator UX.
MASTER_PLAN.md:176 says every generic GUI feature must pass the managed-operations filter.

findings.title: Accounts contains stale lifecycle-deferred copy even though live validate/hold/release actions are implemented.

evidence.observed: The live Accounts subtitle still says lifecycle actions are deferred, and the static Accounts table footer says read-only lifecycle deferred. The live banner copy is more accurate because it says hold/release are bounded action requests. The current live Accounts implementation admits bounded per-row validate, hold, and release.

canon_basis: CANON.md:36 requires one active truth surface per topic.
CANON.md:37 forbids split-brain between docs and runtime; this gap is the UI-copy version of that risk.

findings.title: Accounts fixture mode shows disabled top-level actions with strong button styling.

evidence.observed: The Add Account and bulk validate controls are disabled, but the primary visual treatment remains prominent enough to be read as an available path in a quick scan.

canon_basis: MASTER_PLAN.md:175 makes operator UX part of the product value, so disabled high-risk controls should not visually read as ready.
The previous action registry records add/onboard as blocked or absent until a later action-gate contour.

findings.title: Accounts search and filter affordances are visible but not wired.

evidence.observed: Startup wiring covers source, fixture picker, navigation, live actions, confirmation, and refresh. No search/filter event wiring is present.

canon_basis: MASTER_PLAN.md:176 says every generic GUI feature must pass the managed-operations filter.
WORKFLOW_OS_V1_2.md:460 through WORKFLOW_OS_V1_2.md:462 prevent fixing decorative UI controls in this contour.

findings.title: Accounts rendering slices to 12 rows without pagination controls, which can hide larger live pool details.

evidence.observed: The UI reports visible count and total count, but renderAccountRows only renders accounts.slice(0, 12), and there is no next/previous control.

canon_basis: CANON.md:42 requires architecture designed for 20 accounts from day one.
MASTER_PLAN.md:194 requires data format and UI readiness for 20 accounts without redesign.

findings.title: Hold/release action results are shown through generic fields only, not through lifecycle-specific owner-packet fields.

evidence.observed: The current action panel renders top-level status, machine code, message, next action, and changed files. COMMAND_API allows nested hold_result/release_result fields that are not surfaced yet.

canon_basis: COMMAND_API.md:886 says hold/release success must not be inferred from subprocess exit alone.
COMMAND_API.md:899 through COMMAND_API.md:920 define optional nested hold/release result fields that can inform later action-ledger rendering.

findings.title: Confirmation modal exposes raw technical booleans and is not yet strong enough for future destructive actions.

evidence.observed: The modal is truthful and generic, but it displays UI action, true/false mutation state, and required/not required refresh state rather than a risk-calibrated operator explanation.

canon_basis: MASTER_PLAN.md:175 makes operator UX part of the product value.
COMMAND_API.md:14 forbids UI fallback parsing; confirmation copy must explain command meaning without inventing extra truth.

findings.title: Healthy fixture state uses green success styling even though the text correctly says it is not runtime truth.

evidence.observed: The copy is explicit, but fixture banners and chips still use success colors. This is acceptable for the current prototype but should be rechecked before desktop transfer.

canon_basis: CANON.md:38 forbids false-green or stale-green status.
The current copy mitigates the issue; this is a low-severity safety gap for later visual treatment.

findings.title: Bulk lifecycle affordances remain deferred while visible table selection suggests future bulk behavior.

evidence.observed: Per-row account action gates are implemented, but bulk selection remains decorative/deferred. This is safe today but must not be accidentally activated without a separate action contract.

canon_basis: MASTER_PLAN.md:372 says operations that change active routing, registry, mode, or managed state must go through one serialized path.
MASTER_PLAN.md:377 says UI and automation use JSON output as the primary interface; bulk lifecycle would need its own admitted JSON command path.
